title: F46-F49 atlas semantic-gap guards + convention doc
trigger: 2026-04-26_atlas_semantic_gap_audit found 21 MISMATCH (9 xpoll-sigma + 11 xpoll-tau + 1 MILL-DFS23) — Notation A vs B conflation needs both prose discipline (convention doc) and machine guards (4 falsifiers)

F46_xpoll_sigma_convention_guard.intent: cleanup-target — emits NO_XPOLL_SIGMA_VIOLATIONS only after the 9 xpoll-sigma entries are migrated to Notation A or canonical B

F47_xpoll_tau_convention_guard.intent: cleanup-target — emits NO_XPOLL_TAU_VIOLATIONS only after the 11 xpoll-tau entries are migrated

F48_function_call_self_reference_guard.composition: 23 xpoll-* (8 sigma + 11 tau + 4 n) + 9 non-xpoll (n=n(6)=6 entries which are SEMANTICALLY CORRECT at the n6 anchor)
F48_function_call_self_reference_guard.intent: regression-blocker — fails if ANY new func(N)=N pattern appears, regardless of function name; pinned at 32 since n=n(6)=6 lines are legitimate

F49_semantic_gap_audit_clean.intent: broad regression-blocker — invokes the audit script, fails if MISMATCH grows; catches semantic gaps F46-F48 cannot pattern-match

tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.claim: no xpoll-* entry uses sigma(N) = N malformed pattern (Notation A misformatted as B)
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.reason: sigma(N)=N where the parenthetical equals the result is a Notation A vs B conflation (e.g. sigma(12)=12 means sigma=12 i.e. σ(6)=12, NOT σ(12)=12; canonical σ(12)=28). 9 xpoll-sigma-* entries currently exhibit the malformed pattern and are tracked for migration. Sentinel emits only AFTER cleanup; until then HIT serves as a build-time reminder.
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.fix: migrate each xpoll-sigma-* entry from `sigma(12) = 12` to either `sigma = 12` (Notation A shorthand) or `sigma(6) = 12` (canonical Notation B). See design/atlas_function_call_convention.md.
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.origin: Ω-cycle 2026-04-26 F46-F49 semantic-gap guards (audit found 21 MISMATCH; 9 sigma-class)

tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.claim: no xpoll-* entry uses tau(N) = N malformed pattern (Notation A misformatted as B)
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.reason: tau(N)=N where the parenthetical equals the result is the same Notation A vs B confusion as F46 (e.g. tau(4)=4 means tau=4 i.e. τ(6)=4, NOT τ(4)=4; canonical τ(4)=3). 11 xpoll-tau-* entries currently exhibit the malformed pattern. Sentinel emits only AFTER cleanup.
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.fix: migrate each xpoll-tau-* entry from `tau(4) = 4` to either `tau = 4` (Notation A) or `tau(6) = 4` (canonical Notation B). See design/atlas_function_call_convention.md.
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.origin: Ω-cycle 2026-04-26 F46-F49 semantic-gap guards (audit found 21 MISMATCH; 11 tau-class)

tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.claim: atlas-wide count of func(N)=N self-referential entries does not exceed baseline 32 (regression blocker for new self-ref violations)
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.cmd: python3 -c "import re; t=0; [t := t+1 for line in open('/Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.n6') for m in [re.search(r'= ([a-zA-Z_]+)\\((\\d+)\\) = (\\d+)', line)] if m and m.group(2)==m.group(3)]; print('SELFREF_BASELINE_OK' if t<=32 else 'SELFREF_REGRESSION_'+str(t))"
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.reason: Broader pattern than F46/F47: any func(N)=N across atlas. Current count = 32 = 8 xpoll-sigma + 11 xpoll-tau + 4 xpoll-n + 9 non-xpoll. The non-xpoll cases (n=n(6)=6, n6atlas L(24)=...) include legitimate identity-at-anchor entries. Pinning at 32 catches NEW additions of either flavour without forcing immediate cleanup of legitimate cases. Decoupled from F46/F47 cleanup: F48 stays PASS even after F46/F47 cleanup (count drops to ≤32).
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.fix: investigate the new func(N)=N entry; if it's a Notation A miswrite, fix per convention doc; if it's a legitimate identity-at-anchor, raise the F48 baseline in this falsifier and document the addition in design/atlas_function_call_convention.md 'Past offenders' or a new 'Legitimate identity entries' section.
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.origin: Ω-cycle 2026-04-26 F46-F49 semantic-gap guards (broader self-ref regression blocker)

tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.claim: atlas semantic-gap audit MISMATCH count does not exceed baseline 21 (regression blocker for any new semantic gap, broader than pattern-only guards)
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.cmd: MM=$(python3 /Users/ghost/core/nexus/design/hexa_sim/2026-04-26_atlas_semantic_gap_audit.py 2>&1 | grep 'MISMATCH:' | awk '{print $2}'); [ "$MM" -le 21 ] && echo SEMANTIC_GAP_BASELINE_OK || echo SEMANTIC_GAP_REGRESSION_$MM
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.reason: F46-F48 catch specific syntactic patterns; F49 invokes the full canonical-evaluation audit (sigma/tau/phi/sopfr/mu/M3 etc. on every `<id> = func(N) = V` entry across atlas + 6 shards) and pins MISMATCH at 21. Catches semantic violations the syntactic guards miss — e.g. `sigma(8) = 14` (canonical 15) would slip past F46 since the parens and result differ, but F49 catches it. As xpoll cleanup lands, baseline shrinks; revise this falsifier when it does.
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.fix: run audit script locally to see the new MISMATCH; cross-check whether it's a wrong-canonical case or a wrong-author case; fix atlas entry per convention doc, then re-run F49 to confirm baseline holds.
tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block.origin: Ω-cycle 2026-04-26 F46-F49 semantic-gap guards (broadest semantic regression blocker)

verification_log.F46_cmd_exit: HIT — 9 sigma violations present, sentinel suppressed (cleanup-target framing)
verification_log.F47_cmd_exit: HIT — 11 tau violations present, sentinel suppressed (cleanup-target framing)
verification_log.F48_cmd_exit: PASS — current 32 ≤ baseline 32, sentinel SELFREF_BASELINE_OK emitted
verification_log.F49_cmd_exit: PASS — current MISMATCH 21 ≤ baseline 21, sentinel SEMANTIC_GAP_BASELINE_OK emitted

queued_followup_omega_cycle.name: xpoll-* cleanup ω-cycle
queued_followup_omega_cycle.rationale: raw 71 SUGGEST mode + atlas content mutation; 20 atlas.n6 lines would change (9 sigma + 11 tau). Cleanup is mechanical (pure rewrite, no semantic change to load-bearing values) but touches the canonical n6 SSOT. Surface-level visibility is now achieved via F46/F47 cleanup-target falsifiers; user can either green-light the rewrite or leave the guards as long-running cleanup reminders. MILL-DFS23 p(6)=11 → partition(6)=11 fix can ride the same cycle.
queued_followup_omega_cycle.scope: 9 xpoll-sigma-* lines + 11 xpoll-tau-* lines + 1 MILL-DFS23 line in /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.n6 = 21 lines

merge_instructions.merge_method: append the 4 tier_1_falsifiers_jsonl_block entries as one JSONL line each; main thread batches per raw 71
